confidence_rationale: Authors' own review of the action-chunking + habit-formation field they helped build. Synthesises the dual-operator framework (Smith & Graybiel 2013, also in this entry set) with broader literature, articulating habit formation as not-a-single-process but a confluence of dissociable substrate-level changes. Useful R2 + R4 review-level support but secondary literature (lower source quality than primary papers in entry set).
failure_signatures: An REE substrate that treats habit formation as a monolithic process will fail Smith & Graybiel's dissociability test: distinct neural-activity changes occur at distinct timepoints in distinct substrates during habit formation. ARC-064 and MECH-312 (and ARC-062 / ARC-065) need to be implemented as separable processes.

If REE's bottom-up rule discovery is implemented as a one-shot extraction operation rather than a multi-stage progression (rapid chunking in extraction substrate -> slow stabilisation in consolidation substrate), it will fail to reproduce the multi-timepoint signature Smith & Graybiel describe.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Habit formation is not a single process but a confluence of dissociable changes in neural activity occurring across multiple brain regions and even within single regions. The basal-ganglia-cortical circuit hosts the central machinery; addictions and OCD reflect dysfunctions in different components of this network. Habits are sculpted from multiple separable substrate-level changes during overtraining.
mapping.ree_translation: ARC-064 (bottom-up rule discovery) and MECH-312 (arbitration) need to be designed as multi-component substrates rather than monolithic mechanisms. The Smith & Graybiel review supplies the conceptual frame: habit formation as multi-stage, multi-substrate, multi-timepoint progression. R3 verdict supplement: the consolidation arm of bottom-up rule discovery is implicit here -- chunked action sequences stabilise into habits over training, with the stabilisation likely involving sleep-replay or otherwise-offline consolidation. Cross-reference Wilson & McNaughton 1994 / Karlsson 2009 / Stickgold 2013 (sleep-replay-consolidation literature) for the offline arm.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Dialogues in Clinical Neuroscience review-format paper, lower source quality than peer-reviewed primary papers. The review summarises an emerging picture rather than testing it; the specific substrate-design implications for REE require extrapolation. The clinical applications (addiction, OCD) are mentioned but not central; transfer to REE's open-ended-foraging context requires care.
mapping.source_context: Dialogues in Clinical Neuroscience review. Smith and Graybiel's own synthesis of the action-chunking-and-habit-formation field they pioneered. Overlaps significantly with their primary papers in the entry set.

confidence_components.notes: Dialogues in Clin Neurosci venue (high-quality clinical review journal but not the highest-tier neuroscience venue). Mapping fidelity moderate -- the multi-stage habit-formation framework is conceptually relevant for ARC-064 + MECH-312 design. Transfer risk moderate.
